name: Zendesk MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Zendesk MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Zendesk's customer service platform — reading, creating, and updating tickets; querying users and organizations; managing macros; and analyzing support queue state.
use_cases: Reading ticket content for AI-assisted support routing and triage
Creating and updating tickets from agent-driven customer interactions
Querying ticket queue state for SLA monitoring agents
Searching user and organization records for context-aware support agents
Running reports on support volume and satisfaction from analytics agents
not_for: Teams using Freshdesk, ServiceNow, or other helpdesk platforms
Real-time live chat handling (use Zendesk's messaging APIs for that)
Complex workflow automation (use Zendesk native triggers and automations)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Zendesk's support ticket system — reading, triaging, or creating tickets as part of a customer support workflow.
avoid_when: You're using a different helpdesk platform — use that platform's integration.

auth.notes: API tokens (email/token pair) for agent use. OAuth 2.0 with scope controls for user-delegated access. Basic auth discouraged. Granular OAuth scopes per resource.

pricing.paid_tiers: Suite Team: $55/agent/month; Suite Growth: $89; Suite Pro: $115
pricing.notes: Premium pricing for enterprise support. API access included in all plans. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: 700 req/min for API token; 200 req/min for OAuth; well-documented

known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit (700 req/min for API token) is generous but agents can hit it with bulk operations
Subdomain required in all API URLs — configure {subdomain}.zendesk.com
Ticket comments vs ticket updates are different API operations
Search API uses Zendesk query syntax — not Elasticsearch or SQL
Deleted tickets have retention period — they may still appear in queries briefly
Custom fields have specific type constraints — agents must validate field types
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth scopes provide good granularity. API tokens are per-agent (email/token pair). SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise-grade support platform security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth scopes and API token per-agent model provide good security. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise-grade.
